Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full Time Permanent
Position Classification: Health Manager Level 4
Remuneration: $147,653 - $175,917 per annum plus Superannuation
Location: 1 Reserve Road, St Leonards NSW
Hours Per Week: 38 hours
Requisition ID:REQ656682

Join our Business Analytics & Intelligence Team (BAIT) at Ministry of Health for an exciting Full Time Permanent opportunity and be part of the team enriching health in millions of ways every day.  

About us

We are committed to your growth and development, providing the resources and opportunities you need to excel in your career.
Join a passionate team dedicated to innovation and creativity.
While regular office presence is essential, we offer flexible work arrangements to support your needs.


To learn more about the Ministry and what benefits we offer, access our Employee Benefits Handbook.

About the role:
With your skills and experience, you can contribute to the millions of ways we’re enhancing health and wellness while developing 
your career as part of the largest health organisation in Australia.

Be part of a team that turns complex financial data into clear, trusted insights that support critical decision making across NSW Health. As a Senior Statistician in Financial Planning and Analytics, you will apply advanced statistical and analytical methods to inform patient costing, funding models and system performance. Your work will help decision makers understand trends, trade offs and impacts across the health system, supporting evidence based planning and resource allocation. You will collaborate with experienced analysts and stakeholders across districts, pillars and the Ministry to ensure insights are robust, transparent and fit for purpose. The role offers exposure to large scale, real world data challenges and the opportunity to deepen your expertise in health system analytics. If you are driven by analytical rigour, relevance and impact, this role provides a meaningful platform to shape better decisions through data.

Health Manager Level 4, starting salary from $147,653 - $175,917 per annum plus Superannuation, with a 38-hour work week.

What you’ll be doing

Extract, transform and statistically analyse large, complex health datasets to support patient costing, funding analysis and system level decision making. 
Work collaboratively with other BAIT team members to reconcile multi source data, resolve data quality issues and ensure analytical consistency. 
Apply advanced statistical techniques to identify trends, variances and cost drivers across activity based funding and patient care streams. 
Produce reproducible, well documented analytical outputs that support routine reporting, modelling and ad hoc analytical requests. 
Translate complex analytical findings into clear, decision ready insights for financial and funding stakeholders across NSW Health.

 

About you:
We are seeking motivated and committed individuals who can support the work of NSW Health with: 

Tertiary qualifications in statistics, health information management, data science or a related quantitative discipline, or equivalent demonstrated experience.
Strong experience extracting, validating and analysing high volume health datasets using tools such as SAS, SQL, Python/R and Excel.
Sound knowledge of patient costing, activity based funding and health system data, with an understanding of data governance and quality frameworks.
Proven ability to work collaboratively with other team members to reconcile multi source data and produce robust, reproducible analysis.
Demonstrated capability to translate complex statistical outputs into clear, decision ready insights for technical and non technical stakeholders.


How to apply
At The Ministry of Health, we want you to make the most of the opportunities you’ll get to grow your skills and your career, surrounded by authentic, smart people and leaders.
Please review the role description and submit your up-to-date resume (up to 5 pages) and cover letter (1-2 pages) AND demonstrate your ability by attaching a response to the two target questions below.

Target Questions (1-2 pages)

Describe your experience extracting, validating and analysing large, multi source datasets to support patient costing or activity based funding and include what tools and techniques did you use, and how did you ensure data quality?
Provide an example of how you worked collaboratively with analytics colleagues to produce robust, reproducible analysis and translate it into clear insights for financial or funding related decision making.


Use the link below to access our Capability Application tool, designed to help you excel in the recruitment process. Practice your responses with the example questions provided to effectively highlight your achievements and the strategies you used to accomplish them.  

Capability application tool | NSW Public Service Commission

For role related queries or questions contact Mauricio Olivera on Mauricio.Olivera@health.nsw.gov.au and quote REQ656682

As an organisation we are committed to creating an accessible and inclusive workplace. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact Anu Kundu on anu.kundu@health.nsw.gov.au

Closing Date: 7 May 2026

CSQ327R185

As a Deployment Strategist (DS), you are the critical bridge between a customer's multi-million dollar business problem and Databricks' technical solution. You are a product manager for the field, owning the entire non-technical lifecycle of an FDE engagement.

You will partner with our Account Executives to build C-suite trust, perform deep discovery to map the customer's political and technical landscape, and, most importantly, scope the value. You will define the Minimum Viable Product for a pilot, write the PRD that guides the engineering team, and manage the project to a successful landing.

This role requires a rare mix of C-suite gravitas, deep product-style thinking, and a hands-on, get it done execution mindset.

The impact you will have:

C-Suite Scoping & Value Definition: Partner with Sales to perform deep discovery, map the customer's organization, quantify the business value ($10M+ problems), and define the pilot (MVP, success criteria, timeline).
End-to-End Product Ownership: Act as the CEO of the project by authoring the PRD, prioritizing the engineering backlog, relentlessly managing scope, and acting as the primary hands-on QA tester.
Agile Project Execution & Delivery: Drive the FDE build team via an agile process, manage all stakeholder communications from the C-suite to individual engineers, and act as the escalation manager to unblock all technical or political issues.
Solution Launch & Landing: Own the Day 0 launch, manage the war room, and drive the solution from launch to full adoption by capturing user feedback, iterating, and proving the defined business value.
Strategic Handoff & Growth: Scope the ongoing maintenance plan, create customer documentation/training, and leverage your trusted advisor status to identify the next high-value FDE engagement.

What we look for:

Executive Presence & Communication: A Trusted Advisor with C-suite gravitas and superb communication skills. You are opinionated, humble, and can build rapport and drive decisions in days, not weeks.
Product & Strategy Mindset: A Product Thinker and Systems Thinker who can perform a SWOT analysis, understand a competitive landscape, and define what to build and why.
Relentless Execution: An Execution Driver with a Hands-On mentality. You are relentless, an expert at escalation, and willing to do the unglamorous work to get the project done.
Ideal Experience: 2+ years as a Deployment Strategist OR 2+ years at a high-growth startup in a builder or founder capacity. (Alternatively: 5+ years in a senior, client-facing product or delivery role at a top-tier tech company or consultancy).
Proven Skills: Demonstrated experience in value-based scoping (TCO/ROI), managing agile software projects, writing PRDs, and the ability/interest to travel up to 50%.

About Databricks

Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on 
Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook

About the Company:
This is an exciting opportunity for a talented Regulatory Reporting Specialist to join a leading multinational brand in the Banking sector. Known for its strong market presence and commitment to excellence, this organisation offers a dynamic and inclusive environment where your technical skills and regulatory expertise will be recognised and nurtured.


You'll be joining a high-performing team of 6 professionals who are passionate about precision, compliance, and collaboration. This role reports directly to the Senior Regulatory Reporting Manager, giving you exposure to senior leadership and the chance to contribute to strategic regulatory initiatives.


Becoming part of this team means aligning with a company that values transparency, compliance, and long-term growth. Your work will directly support the organisation's commitment to regulatory excellence and financial integrity.

About the role:

Prepare and lodge accurate and timely regulatory reports in accordance with APRA, and other standards.
Interpret and apply evolving regulatory requirements, ensuring internal processes and reporting remain compliant.
Perform variance analysis and reconciliations to validate data integrity across regulatory submissions.
Collaborate with Finance, Risk, and Treasury teams to gather and validate data inputs for reporting.
Support the implementation of regulatory changes, including system updates and process enhancements.
Assist in the preparation of responses to regulatory queries and audits.
Contribute to automation and process improvement initiatives to enhance reporting efficiency and accuracy.
Maintain up-to-date documentation of reporting processes and controls.

About you:

CA or CPA qualified with 3-5 years of experience in regulatory reporting or financial control within the Banking or Financial Services industry.
Strong understanding of APRA reporting standards and frameworks.
Experience with regulatory reporting tools and systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle) is highly desirable.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a high attention to detail.
Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively across departments.
Proactive mindset with a continuous improvement approach.


If you're looking to take the next step in your career and make a meaningful impact in a well-established financial institution, this is the perfect opportunity for you.


To apply directly for this exciting role, follow the prompts as directed. For more information or a confidential discussion, contact Raelene Horn at Mane Consulting.

Company Description

Let’s create a more sociable future together

At Endeavour, we’re totally into what we do. With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, we love to bring people together. Job Description
Be uniquely you, come as you are 
Execute pricing vision & strategic agenda within BWS
An exciting opportunity to combine your strong analytical, pricing & strategic skills

As our Pricing Strategy Manager for BWS, you'll execute the strategic direction and governance of retail pricing across the brand.

It's about getting that balance right: keeping us profitable, staying competitive, and driving growth. You'll put our pricing frameworks into action, backed up by solid market smarts and data.

Work closely with other teams to make sure our pricing plays nicely with BWS's bigger commercial goals.

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

Drive BWS pricing strategy, aligning with commercial, customer, and brand goals to boost profitability and competitiveness.
Partner with stakeholders to embed pricing as a core commercial lever, balancing value with customer experience.
Lead pricing innovation through a test-and-learn agenda, generating insights for continuous optimisation.
Collaborate on promotional pricing frameworks to maximise ROI and relevance.
Utilise analytics for BWS-specific insights, identifying inconsistencies, margin opportunities, and performance gaps.
Support pricing mechanics and customer offers by partnering with Loyalty, Digital, and Ecommerce teams.
Monitor BWS pricing performance against financial targets and customer strategies.
Improve operational efficiency through process innovation and capability uplift in pricing.
Act as the BWS pricing representative, building strategic partnerships to embed pricing into decision-making and gain alignment in a matrix environment.
Role model Endeavour Group’s Leadership Values and support the broader Customer Value agenda.
Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you: 

5+ years of experience in revenue management or pricing strategy, with a proven track record of success.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or similar.
Proven strategy implementation experience.
Strong analytical skills, including proficiency in financial modeling and data analysis tools.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey pricing strategies to diverse stakeholders.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.
Experience with market research and competitive analysis.
Retail experience is highly regarded.

The benefits are good too! 

We offer hybrid ways of working
An exclusive discount card for BWS, Dan Murphy’s, Woolworths, BIG W and other Endeavour Group brands, including our ALH pubs
Your health and wellbeing is your most important asset, and as one of our valued team members, it’s our first priority. You will have a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing
Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big.

At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

Additional Information

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.  

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

AML Data Scientists (Multiple Levels) | Financial Crime Change & Remediation Programme

A leading financial services organisation is building out a high-performing team of AML Data Scientists to support a large-scale financial crime change and remediation programme.
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of financial crime prevention, leveraging advanced analytics and AI to enhance surveillance capabilities across AML, sanctions, and customer due diligence.

 What you’ll be doing
Analyse financial crime trends and identify emerging risks across large, complex datasets
Develop and deploy machine learning and predictive models to enhance detection capabilities
Apply advanced analytics, NLP, and GenAI techniques to improve transaction monitoring and surveillance outcomes
Support ongoing remediation and uplift initiatives, driving improvements across systems and controls
Collaborate with risk, compliance, and technology teams to deliver scalable, data-driven solutions
What we’re looking for
Experience in data science / machine learning roles within financial services or regulated environments
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL
Exposure to financial crime / AML / transaction monitoring / KYC / sanctions (highly regarded)
Experience with NLP, LLMs, GenAI, or advanced analytics techniques
Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights
Nice to have:

Experience with SAS, Power BI, or similar tools
Exposure to deep learning, reinforcement learning, or AI model deployment
Familiarity with tools such as OpenAI, Microsoft Cognitive Services (e.g. speech-to-text, text mining, image recognition)

About the Company

Our client is a large, customer‑focused organisation operating at national scale and undergoing significant transformation.

About the Role

Our client is establishing a greenfield AI capability and is seeking an AI Transformation Lead to operate at the intersection of business transformation, AI solution delivery, and change management.

This role is not hands‑on engineering. Instead, you will work closely with business units, subject matter experts and engineering teams to identify AI opportunities, redesign workflows for human + AI collaboration, and ensure solutions are successfully adopted and optimised post‑launch.

The focus is on embedding AI into real operational and customer environments at scale, delivering measurable outcomes rather than proofs of concept.

Key Responsibilities:


Identify and shape high‑value AI use cases across operations, internal productivity and customer experiences.
Translate business problems into clear AI solution definitions and delivery plans.
Redesign end‑to‑end workflows to embed human‑AI collaboration.
Partner closely with AI engineering teams to guide solution delivery.
Lead adoption, training and change activities to embed AI into BAU.
Establish feedback loops and drive continuous optimisation post‑launch.
Track benefits and outcomes including efficiency, cost and experience improvements.
Engage confidently with senior stakeholders across business and technology.

About You


Significant prior experience delivering digital, AI or technology‑enabled transformation in complex organisations.
Proven experience taking AI or automation initiatives from concept through to production and adoption.
Strong ability to translate business challenges into structured AI use cases.
Experience redesigning workflows and embedding new ways of working.
Solid conceptual understanding of modern AI (e.g. LLMs, RAG, agentic workflows, prompting).
Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and delivery skills.
Backgrounds from consulting, transformation, product or innovation roles are well suited.
Technical/development background (Advantageous)

The Benefits


Permanent opportunity to shape a greenfield AI function
High‑visibility role with strong executive backing
Hybrid working arrangement
Competitive salary package
Long‑term career growth as the AI capability scales

This is not a traditional BA role. You will operate at a strategic level, leading engagement across complex programs, influencing senior stakeholders, and providing commercial insights that guide large-scale infrastructure, research, and student experience initiatives.

About You:

Proven experience delivering business cases and commercial advisory across large-scale programs
Strong financial modelling and analytical capability
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
Experience working across commerce, finance, or education industries

Solve by Talent is partnering with the University of Sydney to recruit a Manager, Research Systems to support the delivery of key Information and Communications Technology (ICT) initiatives that help shape the future of education, research, and innovation. 
  
Why join the University of Sydney? 
  
Join one of Australia’s leading universities at a pivotal point of change, where technology is evolving to better support student learning, empower teaching excellence, and enable research delivery at global scale. 
  
ICT is central to delivering the University’s academic mission. Working across faculties, disciplines, and research communities, you will shape the platforms, services, and partnerships that enable student success, strengthen teaching, and accelerate research impact. 
  
This work spans both the critical services that keep the University running every day and the ongoing improvement of systems and experiences. 
  
With strong investment and clear direction, you’ll operate in a large and complex environment where your contribution has visible, meaningful impact across the University. 
  
You’ll collaborate across academic and professional communities to help shape a more connected and capable University, supporting teaching excellence, research impact, and student success. 
  
The opportunity 
  
As Manager, Research Systems, you will lead the strategic management and ongoing improvement of the University’s research administration systems, supporting areas such as grants, ethics, academic profiles, research outputs and related operational processes. This is a newly created leadership role with the opportunity to shape both the function and the team as the University modernises a complex legacy landscape. 
  
You will play a key role in operationalising myResearch Sydney, a flagship Digital Sydney initiative. More than a replacement for IRMA, myResearch Sydney is a new integrated research administration system designed to simplify the research lifecycle by bringing together the people, processes and technology that support research administration, with staged delivery informed by close consultation with researchers and professional staff. 
  
What you’ll do 

Lead the roadmap, support, enhancement, and lifecycle management of the University’s research systems  
Manage a specialist team responsible for application support, maintenance, and continuous improvement  
Partner with research stakeholders and ICT teams to improve integration, service quality, and user experience  
Support the delivery and operationalisation of My Research Sydney and related initiatives  
Oversee governance, reporting, prioritisation, and issue resolution across the research systems portfolio  
What we’re looking for 

Experience leading platforms, systems, or application services in a complex ICT environment
Strong people leadership skills, with the ability to build capability and lead through change 
Background in service management, operational support, and continuous improvement 
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including the ability to work across technical and business teams 
Experience in a research-intensive, higher education, or similarly complex environment is beneficial  
What’s in it for you? 
  
At the University of Sydney, we support our staff with a range of benefits designed to enhance your career, wellbeing and work-life balance, including: 

Up to 17% superannuation to support your financial future  
Extremely generous parental leave entitlements  
Competitive salary with salary packaging options  
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements  
Career development and learning opportunities  
Health and wellbeing programs including Fitness Passport  
Access to a vibrant, globally recognised university community  
How to apply 
  
If you’re looking for an opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects while helping shape the future of research technology in a world-class university environment, we would love to hear from you. 
  
Accessibility and inclusion 

The University of Sydney is committed to creating an inclusive recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require adjustments or additional support at any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know. 
  
Pre-employment checks 

Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all role required pre-employment or background checks in terms satisfactory to the University. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment. 
  
Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. 
  
Sponsorship / work rights for Australia 
  
You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Manager within the risk function supporting the AUSPAC Modernisation Program, you will play a critical role in enabling transformation across QBE AUSPAC. This role is responsible for overseeing both delivery and delivered risk, ensuring effective risk management throughout implementation and post‑delivery.




About QBE 

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.




Your New Role

Develop and embed project risk management strategies across delivery and delivered risk, ensuring mitigation plans are practical, time‑bound, and actionable.

Proactively monitor project risks and the effectiveness of mitigation plans and controls, maintaining accurate risk registers and providing regular, high‑quality reporting.

Lead comprehensive project risk assessments in partnership with risk owners and project leaders across all project phases.

Clearly articulate risk positions, assessments, and mitigation strategies to stakeholders across the program and QBE AUSPAC business units.

Oversee risk trends and emerging issues, transforming insights into concise, actionable reporting for senior management and governance forums.

Collaborate closely with project and business teams to promote strong risk awareness and provide guidance on effective risk management practices and tools.




About You

A strong transformation mindset, with the ability to operate effectively in complex, high‑change environments.

Proven experience managing transformation risk across large‑scale, business‑led change programs, covering both delivery and delivered risk.

Demonstrated experience within the insurance or broader financial services industry, with a sound understanding of operating in regulated environments.

The ability to manage multiple priorities while remaining focused, structured, and outcome‑driven.

Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to articulate complex matters clearly and succinctly at senior levels.

A tertiary qualification in economics, accounting, business, or a related discipline (formal risk qualifications are not essential).




Benefits 

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Free holistic wellbeing coaching, nutritional, confidential counselling, financial and legal advice

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

Junior-Mid Level | Financial Technology
 
expertech are seeking a Quantitative Software Engineer to join our growing team at Elevia. This is a hands-on role for someone with a strong academic foundation in mathematics or a related quantitative discipline who wants to apply first-principles thinking to real financial problems.
You will work closely with senior engineers and the firm's principals to help build and maintain the data and application infrastructure that underpins our investment and analytics platform. This role offers broad exposure across the full development lifecycle, from system design to implementation and ongoing optimisation in a technically rigorous, intellectually curious environment.

Essential Skills & Experience

Strong programming ability in Python, with deep familiarity with the data science ecosystem (e.g. pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn)
Excellent academic background in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or Physics
Postgraduate qualifications (Master's or PhD) are highly regarded
Solid understanding of database systems (SQL Server, PostgreSQL), including data modelling, performance optimisation, and data integrity
Experience building or maintaining data pipelines and backend systems for analytical or data-intensive applications
A rigorous, first-principles approach to problem-solving
Ability to work independently, exercise sound judgement, and operate effectively with minimal supervision
Australian working rights (essential)
Desirable Skills & Experience

2-4 years' experience in a large financial institution, quantitative firm, or similarly data-driven environment
Prior exposure to financial markets, trading systems, accounting data, or financial modelling
Experience integrating external financial data sources (e.g. Bloomberg, Refinitiv, proprietary feeds)
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure, particularly AWS
Experience with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and production system monitoring
On Offer

You will lead market risk governance and independent oversight across physical and financial commodity trading activities spanning agriculture, energy, carbon, base and precious metals, and electricity markets. Requirements:

Excellent tertiary qualifications in an Applied Mathematical or Financial discipline.
At least 5 years' experience within the Market Risk domain.
Sound understanding of both physical and financial commodity markets, including delivery logistics, storage, basis risk and operational risk considerations.

Experience designing and overseeing monitoring frameworks, incorporating KRIs, KPIs, performance triggers and early warning indicators.

Together building prosperity in the Kimberley and beyond — Safety, Sustainability, Community: it’s in our DNA.
 

At KMS, success is not just what we do — it’s how we do it. We’re seeking a hands on, outcomes focused Principal Continuous Improvement to embed a culture of improvement and lift performance across the business. You’ll be a trusted advisor to senior leaders and own enterprise level programs that strengthen governance, sustainability and stakeholder confidence.

About the Project

Kimberley Mineral Sands Pty Ltd (KMS) is a 50/50 joint venture between Sheffield Resources Limited and YGH Australia Investment Pty Ltd (Yansteel). The Thunderbird Mineral Sands Operation (Thunderbird) is located mid-way between Derby and Broome, in the Kimberley region of Western Australia. 

Thunderbird is one of the largest and highest-grade zircon mineral sands deposits in the world and contains valuable heavy minerals including ilmenite, zircon, leucoxene, and rutile, which are extracted and exported via the Port of Broome. 

Our head office is based in Broome, and together we are focused on delivering economic and social benefits for the West Kimberley and its communities. We do this through living our values of Integrity, Accountability, Humility, Co-Creation and Inclusivity. 

About the Role

Reporting to the Head of Operations, this is an exciting opportunity for a Principal Continuous Improvement to join our growing mining company.

This role can be offered on a residential Bus In Bus Out (BIBO) or Fly In Fly Out (FIFO) arrangement. Relocation support and additional benefits will be provided should the successful candidate be interested in moving to the West Kimberley.

What you’ll lead

Enterprise Continuous Improvement (CI) framework: design, implement and monitor a practical improvement framework aligned to organisational strategy; track outcomes and benefits with clear reporting.
Stewardship (organisational “wiring”) Program: own the design, governance and activities cadence (balanced scorecards, dashboards, KPIs) to drive accountability, transparency and responsible resource management.
Priority improvement delivery: identify and prioritise opportunities; facilitate CI activities such as value stream mapping, problem solving and idea generation; lead compliant process enhancements and sustainment plans.
Culture & capability: coach and mentor leaders and teams in CI methods (Lean, Six Sigma); develop tools, templates and training that build capability.
HS&E and risk alignment: ensure initiatives align with policies, risk frameworks, compliance requirements and our zero harm expectations.

About You

Degree in Business, Management or related discipline.
8+ years’ experience in continuous improvement, process optimisation or organisational development in complex environments.
Certification in Lean/Six Sigma or equivalent experience.
Strong analytics with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
Highly effective stakeholder engagement and communication; you build trust quickly and collaborate to deliver meaningful change.
Leadership qualities that fit our environment: resilience and adaptability, a practical problem solving mindset, persistence and strong emotional intelligence.

Why join KMS?

Impact at enterprise scale: shape systems that improve performance and stewardship across the business.
Exposure & trust: partner closely with senior leaders; your advice and delivery will be visible and valued.
Community & purpose: contribute to prosperity in the Kimberley and beyond, grounded in safety and sustainability

Our Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

KMS is committed to advancing an inclusive and diverse workforce culture and environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We acknowledge the different skills, experiences, and perspectives that people from diverse backgrounds including, race, ethnicity, disability, age, gender identity and sexual orientation bring to the workplace, will only strengthen our workforce.

We strongly encourage applications from our key priority communities – Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Ability to work in a fast-paced environment across multiple geographies and manage multiple priorities.

Company Overview

Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the #1 company in e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM).

What you'll do

Docusign is in the midst of a significant transformation. We've evolved beyond e-signature into Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) — an AI-powered platform that helps organisations create, commit to, and manage agreements more intelligently. For our public sector customers, this means faster service delivery, stronger compliance, reduced risk, and better citizen outcomes.

We're looking for a Lead Solution Consultant to join our ANZ team with a dedicated focus on Australian Public sector accounts. You'll partner with our Enterprise sales team to solve complex government challenges by translating Public Sector initiatives, processes, and technology requirements into compelling IAM solutions.

This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Manager, Solution Consulting.


Responsibility

Act as the technical lead on Federal Government and Public Sector accounts in partnership with Enterprise Sales across ANZ, owning the Technical strategy from discovery through to close

Conduct deep discovery workshops with executive and senior government stakeholders to understand organisational challenges, policy drivers, and technology landscapes

Translate complex government requirements — including compliance, security, accessibility, and interoperability needs — into tailored IAM solution designs

Inform account planning with strategic technical insights that shape the overall customer strategy and execution approach

Build and deliver compelling product demonstrations, whiteboard sessions, proof-of-concept engagements, and workshops customised to public sector use cases

Respond to RFPs, RFIs, and Tenders with clear, compliant, and persuasive technical narratives

Communicate recommendations with confidence using value messaging frameworks, storytelling, customer evidence, and visually compelling materials

Collaborate closely with Sales, Professional Services, Customer Success, Product, Marketing and Leadership to maximise impact across customer engagements

Champion Docusign as a trusted, secure, and effective partner for government agreement processes

Stay current on public sector technology trends, government digital transformation programs, and relevant regulatory/compliance frameworks (e.g., PSPF, IRAP, ISM, NZ GCDO guidelines)

Share insights, learnings, and reusable assets across the team and broader organisation to drive scale and collaboration

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Big transformation. Real impact. Immediate start.

This is your chance to step into a high-profile enterprise transformation program, helping deliver a major shift to a modern, cloud-based SAP platform. If you enjoy working in complex environments and know how to drive change that actually sticks this role is worth a look.

The Role

You'll take ownership of end-to-end change and communications delivery across a large-scale program, supporting the rollout of new systems, processes, and ways of working.

This is a hands-on role ideal for someone who enjoys being in the detail while still influencing at a senior level.

What you'll be doing

Drive change planning and delivery across the full project lifecycle
Develop practical, high-quality artefacts (impact assessments, comms plans, readiness plans)
Deliver clear, engaging communications to diverse stakeholder groups
Partner with stakeholders to embed change and improve adoption
Support business readiness, training, and transition activities
Work closely with senior leaders to influence and guide outcomes

The Environment

You'll be working on a major finance transformation program, moving from legacy systems to SAP S/4HANA Cloud and introducing more standardised, efficient ways of working.

SAP experience is highly regarded - but not essential.
What matters most is your ability to deliver change in complex environments and bring stakeholders on the journey.

What you'll bring

Strong experience in change management within complex programs
Proven ability to deliver end-to-end change outcomes (not just frameworks)
Confidence working with senior stakeholders and navigating ambiguity
Excellent communication skills - you know how to land a message
Experience in large-scale system or digital transformation environments
Exposure to ERP or SAP programs is a bonus, not a requirement

Why this role?

$123PH inc super
7-month contract with immediate impact and chance to extend
Hybrid working flexibility
Work on a program that will elevate your experience and profile

If you're a delivery-focused Change Analyst who can cut through complexity and drive real outcomes, apply now or reach out to ray.stewart@talentinternational.com

About Us

We're a Brisbane-based software company, delivering software solutions to the travel sector at large for over 25 years.

Our analytics platforms drive decision-making for travel sector clients worldwide.

We are a measured company that values professionalism, providing quality services whilst enjoying work life balance and looking to tread lightly on the planet.

The Role

We're looking for a sharp, technically capable Business Analyst to work closely with our Software Development Manager and act as a link between our customers and development team.

This is not a passive documentation role, you'll be expected to think, challenge, clarify, and drive outcomes.

What You'll Be Doing

Engage directly with customers to understand business needs and translate them into clear, actionable requirements

Analyse data and workflows to identify problems and opportunities for improvement

Produce high-quality functional specifications and supporting documentation for developers

Support the development lifecycle from concept through delivery and release

Provide ongoing customer support and issue resolution

Contribute to team coordination and operational management tasks

What You Bring

A Bachelor degree within the Information Technology domain

3-4 years of Business Analyst experience in a software development environment

Solid database skills (SQL proficiency key)

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capability

Proven experience in requirements gathering and documentation in a software environment

Confidence working with both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders

Ability to manage multiple priorities without losing attention to detail

Highly Regarded

Experience in the travel industry

Familiarity with Global Distribution Systems (GDS, Amadeus, Travelport and Sabre)

Exposure to data analytics platforms or reporting systems

Why This Role

Work on globally relevant products from Brisbane

Join a team that delivers in a competitive international market

Real ownership and influence, your work directly shapes the product evolution

Tight-knit, technically strong team with minimal bureaucracy

Apply

If you're someone who can cut through ambiguity, ask the right questions, and turn complexity into clarity, this role will suit you.

Please send your CV and a brief cover note outlining relevant experience.

HOW TO APPLY

Not all BA roles are equal.

Some are documentation-heavy, slow-moving, and sit far away from actual delivery.

This isn’t one of those.

We're looking for a digitally focused Business Analyst who can operate close to the product, work hands-on with delivery teams, and play a key role in shaping and releasing customer-facing platforms for our retail client.

What you’ll actually be doing
Working across ecommerce, digital platforms, and retail systems
Translating business needs into clear, actionable user stories and requirements
Partnering with product, engineering, and UX to shape customer journeys and digital experiences
Supporting delivery end-to-end from discovery through to release
Playing a key role in testing (UAT, test cases, defect management) to ensure high-quality releases
Running workshops, refining backlogs, and keeping stakeholders aligned
Identifying gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve digital performance
What we’re looking for
Proven experience as a Business Analyst in digital / ecommerce / customer-facing environments
Strong experience working in Agile delivery teams
Comfortable owning UAT, testing coordination, and quality assurance
Ability to bridge the gap between business and technical teams
Experience with tools like Jira, Confluence (or similar)
Someone who can think beyond requirements and contribute to better outcomes
What makes this role different
You won’t just be handed requirements you’ll help shape how things are built
You’ll be working on real customer-facing platforms, not internal admin systems
You’ll have visibility across product, delivery, and business stakeholders
Fast-paced, collaborative environment where things actually move
Who this suits
A BA who enjoys being close to the product and delivery
Someone who’s comfortable getting into the detail but can also step back and see the bigger picture
A BA who doesn’t just document but challenges, improves, and delivers
The client are looking to move quickly with interview's being conducted over the next week. If you’re interested, apply or reach out for a confidential chat.

Sydney or Melbourne

Permanent, full-time position

Hybrid role, a mix of in office and work from home




The opportunity 

As a National Product Specialist, you’ll support the end‑to‑end execution of the Retail product lifecycle across governance, disclosure, portfolio insights and system/process delivery. Working closely with Portfolio and National Portfolio Managers, as well as Disclosure and Product Governance teams, you’ll help ensure Retail products are well governed, customer‑aligned, and ready for execution. This is a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role suited to someone who enjoys coordinating detail, partnering across teams and getting outcomes delivered. 




About QBE 

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.




Your new role 

Deliver delegated BAU product governance activities, including complaints monitoring, product value metrics, product feedback coordination and evidence management

Support execution of Retail Product governance rhythms and artefacts, including review packs, evidence collation and status reporting

Coordinate and progress regulatory and PDS updates in close partnership with Disclosure teams, and support reviews of customer‑facing documentation

Undertake product and wording reviews, along with competitor and market analysis, to support decision‑making and uplift opportunities

Deliver system‑focused product execution work, including analysis, testing support, controls support and stakeholder coordination, and support underwriting audits

Support working groups and committees by preparing materials, tracking actions and ensuring follow‑through, while building strong day‑to‑day partnerships across Product, Portfolio, Underwriting, Pricing, Disclosure and Product Governance




About you 

Experience in insurance across underwriting, product, portfolio, governance, disclosure, or adjacent roles

Exposure to short‑tail retail lines (e.g. Motor, Property) or similar portfolios is desirable but not essential

Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, with comfort working through ambiguity

Proven ability to collaborate with multiple stakeholders to deliver practical outcomes

Highly organised with strong prioritisation skills, able to manage competing deadlines without compromising quality

Clear and confident communicator, able to translate complex detail into concise, audience‑appropriate updates




Benefits 

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Free holistic wellbeing coaching, nutritional, confidential counselling, financial and legal advice

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

To learn more about benefits of working with us, click here.




Rewards & Recognition 

We value our employee’s experience with us and are proud to have been recognised for the following awards:

2025 Winner of Excellence in Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Award at the Insurance Business Australia Awards for our Respect@QBE program

2023 Most Inclusive Workplace at the Australian HR Institute (AHRI) Awards

Ranked in the top ten in the AAGE Top Graduate Employers 2025 for medium sized program as voted by graduates

To learn more about our achievements, click here.




Apply now and let’s make it happen! 

If you’re looking for a career that combines your expertise and your empathy, click Apply today.

Your career. At the heart of it.

Successful applicants will need to complete work rights, police and credit checks through our online provider as a condition of employment.  




QBE recognise the value of diverse perspectives and experience. We are committed to an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected for who they are. If we can provide support with access requirements, alternative work arrangements or you would like to connect with one of our networks please contact us at recruitment@qbe.com

Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The role



The Business Analyst partners with business and technology stakeholders to understand business goals, analyse problems and opportunities, and translate needs into clear requirements, early designs, and prototypes. The role supports the delivery of user-centred digital solutions by shaping product and process improvements, validating concepts early, and enabling efficient delivery through well-defined user stories, acceptance criteria, and solution options.



The team



APRA's Technology team delivers, maintains, and enhances essential information technology services that support the organisation's productivity, strategic objectives, and business unit priorities.

Technology partner with stakeholders to design, implement and maintain IT solutions that meet the needs of the organisation and ensuring these are secure and aligned with APRA's technology architecture.

Key accountabilities


Build strong relationships with business stakeholders to quickly understand objectives, pain points and desired outcomes, and identify where digital products and solutions can add value.

Translate business needs into functional and non-functional requirements, ensuring they are testable, feasible and aligned to outcomes.

Produce high-quality analysis artefacts such as user stories, acceptance criteria, process maps, and impact assessments to support delivery.

Facilitate solution option analysis including costs, benefits, risks, dependencies, and delivery impacts to inform decision-making.

Collaborate with business and technical teams to create early design concepts (e.g., wireframes, workflow diagrams, low-fidelity screens).

Develop and iterate prototypes to validate requirements, user journeys, and usability with stakeholders and end users early in the lifecycle.

Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning and execution, including test scenarios, defect triage, and acceptance sign-off.

Develop and maintain system, training, and user support resources to drive adoption and meet user needs and expectations.

Communicate changes clearly and tailor messages and materials to different audiences, including non-technical stakeholders.




About you


Demonstrated experience as a Technical Business Analyst working in digital delivery environments, with strong capability across discovery, requirements elicitation and high‑quality documentation.

Strong experience delivering digital solutions across platforms such as Microsoft 365, Power Platform, ServiceNow and Oracle Fusion, with hands‑on technical understanding to shape solution design, integrations and delivery outcomes.

Experience facilitating user research, journey mapping and applying human‑centred design approaches.

Proven experience delivering initiatives across multiple digital domains, including web platforms, workflow, automation and enterprise business products.

Solid understanding of end-to-end product lifecycle, digital architecture, agile methodologies and IT service delivery best practices.

Excellent facilitation, communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence outcomes and gain agreement across diverse business and technical stakeholders.

Demonstrated ability to manage priorities, driving outcomes in an agile, business-focused, collaborative environment.




To work with us you need to be an Australian Citizen and have the ability to gain a Defence Clearance.



About APRA

Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) was established in 1998 as an independent statutory authority that supervises almost 1,200 financial institutions that manage $8.6 trillion in assets for Australians across the banking, insurance and superannuation sectors.

In overseeing the safety, competitiveness and stability of the financial system, we seek to recruit, develop and retain highly skilled professionals, who want to help shape financial services and protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. Our employee base of almost 900 come predominantly from the commercial financial services industry or other government agencies; as such, we have the feel of a small corporate organisation that can work flexibly and with agility.

At APRA, we're committed to providing an inclusive workplace where everyone belongs, feels valued and respected. We aspire to attract and foster diversity of background, thought, and experience, recognising that a broad range of perspectives, approaches and ideas makes us stronger, and better enables us to meet our obligation to protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, please inform at application stage so we can do our best to accommodate your requirements.

Analytics Manager required to lead a team of Analysts and BI Developers to deliver insights at an enterpise scale for a leading financial services business.

Responsibilities:

leading a well-established Analytics team, providing support and guidance to members
partnering with Finance, Operations, Marketing and Commercial teams to educate around available analytical tools and dashboards
presenting insights and outcome-focused recommendations to business leaders 
assisting in uplifting the enterprise data warehouse
Requirements:

extensive data analytics and architecture background, with proven experience leading analytical teams
strong stakeholder management skills, with experience gathering requirements and presenting insights and recommendations to drive decision-making
strong SQL, Python and Power BI skills
experience working with Snowflake and dbt
excellent data modelling and management skills
must have strong investment or fund management experience
Click on the 'Apply' button or send your resume to sahuja@fourquarters.com.au

Your Next Great Opportunity: Making a Global Impact in Regenerative Medicine

At Maverick Bio, we are not just building a business, we are saving and improving lives. Our innovative biomaterials drive advances in regenerative medicine and medical devices worldwide.

As our Finance Business Partner, you will play a pivotal role supporting performance across our manufacturing and operations. Partnering closely with operational and leadership teams, you will provide costing insight, drive performance, and enable data-led decision-making as the business continues to grow.

Location: This role can be based in our Dubbo or our Brisbane Office (Eight Mile Plains - parking included).

Why Join Maverick Bio?

Real Impact, Unexpected Place

Contribute to life-saving procedures and ground-breaking health research
Make a difference in a business whose products impact lives globally

Real People, Real Work

Join a down-to-earth, collaborative team that values initiative and action
Experience the freedom to think deeply, influence decisions, and drive real outcomes

Real Growth

Partner closely with leaders
Shape costing, reporting, and performance as the business scales

What You Will Do

Partner with Operations teams and leaders to provide financial support
Analyse business and operational data to provide insights that inform strategic decisions
Deliver high-quality reports and dashboards on performance
Design and maintain financial models and benchmarking tools
Own and maintain standard costing, including variance analysis and cost trends
Deliver cost analysis across materials, labour, overheads, and production inputs
Lead and support cost accounting, budgeting and forecasting processes
Manage inventory accounting, including stock provisions and reconciliations
Identify cost drivers, inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve operational performance
Identify and implement ERP (Business Central) and data system improvements
Lead cross-functional improvement initiatives using lean methodologies
Advise the CEO, Head of Finance, and leadership team on performance-related projects

What We Are Looking For

Minimum 5 years’ experience in Finance Business Partnering, management accounting, or operational finance
Experience in manufacturing, supply chain, or a product-based environment
Strong business partnering capability with the ability to influence and build financial capability, elevating decision-making across non-financial stakeholders
Strong analytical and data interpretation skills
Expertise in financial modelling, cost analysis, and performance management
Solid understanding of inventory accounting and standard costing
Proficiency in ERP systems (Business Central), Power BI, and management reporting tools
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics or related field

Desirable

CPA/CA/CFA or Master’s degree
Experience in data visualisation, automation, or predictive analytics
Exposure to benchmarking and performance analysis
Proven experience leading change and cross-functional improvement projects

Who You Are

Commercially minded with a strong sense of ownership and accountability
Comfortable operating in a hands-on, operational environment
Analytical and curious, with the ability to translate data into practical recommendations
Confident engaging and influencing stakeholders across the business
Structured and detail-oriented, while maintaining a focus on outcomes
Motivated by continuous improvement and driving better business performance

Ready to Take the Lead?

Join a company that embraces innovation, fosters growth, and makes a real difference globally.

Click Apply Now to submit your resume and cover letter.

Have questions? Contact our friendly People and Culture Team on (02) 6885 1200 for a confidential conversation.

Equal Opportunity at Maverick Bio

We celebrate diversity and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Maverick Bio is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourages applications from individuals of all abilities, genders, races, religions, and orientations.

Ad Text:
Business Analyst Specialist

Contract to 27/11/2026 | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland statutory authority to recruit experienced Principal Data Analyst. In this role, you will provide strategic advice on information management issues, strategies, frameworks and support models that will underpin best practice and effective lifecycle management of departmental records and information.

Key responsibilities:

Design, implement, and optimize document libraries, metadata structures, taxonomies, and SharePoint/Teams workspaces.

Create and maintain workflows, permissions, and access controls to ensure secure document collaboration.

Lead or assist in the migration of documents from legacy repositories (e.g. eDOCS) to Microsoft 365, ensuring minimal disruption and data integrity.

Map metadata and ensure appropriate classification, tagging, and storage of migrated content.

Develop, maintain and foster a commitment to quality customer service which you will demonstrate by providing advice on the development, design and operation of contemporary information management systems with a focus on the M365 platform.

Consult and participate in the development and implementation of policy, procedures, standards, information classification and labelling, user access controls, information sharing protocols and other necessary frameworks that will enable the effective operation of IM.

What we’re looking for?

5+ years of experience in Microsoft 365 (SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive) configuration and administration.

Experience with document management systems such as eDOCS.

Experience with cloud-based record management platforms such as RecordPoint.

Detailed knowledge of current and emerging Information Management trends, standards and practices including content service platforms, use of Microsoft platform and collaboration tools and managing their impact on IM.

Establishes and maintains co-operative working relationships with co-workers and stakeholders.

Demonstrated ability to work with a diverse set of stakeholders to raise departmental-wide awareness and adoption of IM standards, that influence the implementation of platforms including case management systems, electronic workflows, electronic document and records management systems.

Analyses information, problems, situations, practices or procedures to define the problem or objective.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact  Ianthe Price on ianthe@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

We’re looking for a strategic and delivery-focused Senior Business Analyst to join a high-impact digital transformation program. Working across digital metering and process transformation initiatives, you’ll play a key role in shaping future-state processes, driving innovation, and ensuring solutions deliver real value for both the business and customers.

You’ll take end-to-end ownership of analysis activities—from early discovery and business case development through to delivery—partnering closely with stakeholders across business and technology teams. This includes facilitating workshops, gathering and defining requirements, supporting solution design, and contributing to change and business readiness activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead requirements gathering, process mapping, and solution analysis activities
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder engagement sessions across the organisation
Develop business cases, cost-benefit analysis, and ROI assessments
Collaborate with IT teams to translate business needs into technical solutions
Support process design, implementation, and continuous improvement initiatives
Contribute to change management and stakeholder communication activities
Key Experience:

Proven experience as a Business Analyst within large-scale transformation programs
Strong process mapping and process improvement capability (e.g. BPMN)
Experience working in complex, cross-functional environments
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Ability to think strategically while delivering to tight timeframes
This is a great opportunity for a proactive and analytical Business Analyst who thrives in fast-paced environments and is passionate about driving meaningful transformation and business outcomes.

Who are we? 
 
Standards Australia (SA) is the peak standards development organisation in Australia, with a rich history that dates back to 1922. Currently, we are undergoing a significant transformation and growth phase. In recent years, our workforce has doubled, and we now proudly employ over 200 professionals dedicated to helping shape Australia’s future and fulfilling our mission of empowering communities. 
 
Our organisation brings experts together to create national and international standards that serve as essential guidelines for products and services, ultimately benefiting the Australian community. Our vision is to be a global leader in providing trusted solutions that improve life today and tomorrow. 

About the role 

We are seeking a Portfolio Scheduling & Reporting Lead to join our Group Project Management Office (GPMO).

Reporting to the Head of GPMO, this role is responsible for maintaining the organisation’s enterprise project schedule and portfolio reporting. You will provide a consolidated view of project timelines, dependencies, risks and resource demand across strategic initiatives, enabling senior leaders to make informed decisions on delivery priorities.
  
What you’ll do

Maintain the enterprise master schedule across organisational projects
Identify and manage cross-project dependencies, sequencing risks and delivery impacts
Support Project Managers and business teams to develop aligned and realistic project schedules
Design and maintain portfolio reporting dashboards and executive reporting
Provide insights on milestones, resource demand and delivery progress
Partner with Finance to support portfolio budget tracking and forecasting
Provide leadership and oversight to the Portfolio Reporting Analyst
About you

You are an experienced PMO or portfolio professional with strong analytical and scheduling capability.

You will bring:

5+ years’ experience in PMO, portfolio scheduling or project controls roles
Experience managing complex multi-project schedules and portfolio reporting
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate delivery data into clear insights
Experience using tools such as MS Project, advanced Excel, Power BI, Jira or Confluence
Experience working within enterprise PMO or programme governance environments
Culture & Benefits 

Standards Australia is proud to have been awarded the prestigious Employer of Choice Award for two consecutive years, 2023 and 2024. This recognition highlights our commitment to creating an exceptional workplace and fostering a culture of excellence. Our iCARE values form the basis of all we do - Integrity, Courage, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence. 
 
We offer a range of People Perks that support flexibility, wellbeing, career growth, and recognition - helping you thrive at work and beyond.

What's next? 
 
If Standards Australia sounds like the place for you, please submit your CV outlining your experience and suitability to this role.
Eligible applicants must be Australian Citizens, Permanent Residents or have legal rights to work in Australia. Shortlisted applicants will be contacted within 15 business days of their application. 

At Standards Australia, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to fostering a working environment that values diversity, inclusion, and belonging. Our mission is to empower our communities, and in doing so, we strive to create an organisation that genuinely reflects and respects the diversity of the communities we serve; putting people at the heart of all we do. We welcome applications from people of all cultural and linguistic groups, people of all ages and genders, people of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander background, people with a disability, and people from the LGBTQIA+ community. Should you need any adjustments to be made to our recruitment process, please contact recruitment@standards.org.au

About the role

QTC is seeking a Principal, Technology Risk and Resilience to provide senior second‑line risk oversight across technology‑enabled change. This role has a strong focus on data governance, data platforms, artificial intelligence (AI), and crisis management, supporting QTC’s evolving Data and Technology Strategy.

Reporting into the Director Technology Risk and Resilience, you will strengthen the capacity of the Risk Division by absorbing senior‑level analysis, challenge, and engagement across complex initiatives. You will operate as a trusted, independent advisor to Technology, Data and Information leadership, providing authoritative risk insight, governance and assurance while remaining clearly independent from first‑line delivery.


Key responsibilities

Provide senior second‑line oversight of technology, data and AI risks across major initiatives

Deliver independent challenge and review of governance, frameworks and key decisions

Act as a subject‑matter expert on data governance and responsible AI use

Influence significant technology and data initiatives early, while remaining delivery‑independent

Support crisis preparedness and technology‑enabled resilience

Provide clear, executive‑level risk insights to senior leaders and risk committees




About you

You are a senior risk professional with deep expertise in technology and data‑related risk, comfortable operating at executive level and providing independent, constructive challenge.

You bring strong technical capability, sound judgement, and credibility as a trusted advisor, alongside the confidence to engage constructively on complex and emerging risk issues.




What you’ll bring

Technical capability

Deep knowledge of technology risk management, with particular expertise in data governance, data platforms, AI risk and emerging technologies

Strong experience assessing complex risk scenarios and translating technical detail into effective governance, controls and assurance

Ability to review, shape and uplift frameworks and standards to support consistent and defensible risk outcomes

Experience applying enterprise risk management frameworks in a second‑line oversight context

Leadership & behavioural capability

Strong integrity and independence, with professional judgement and discretion

Clear, confident communication with senior stakeholders, adapting style to audience and context

Proactive identification of emerging risks and opportunities for improvement

Ability to lead through influence and expertise, particularly in complex or high‑pressure environments

A collaborative mindset, contributing to continuous improvement and team capability




Qualifications & experience

Essential

Senior experience in technology, data or information security risk

Strong second‑line risk oversight and senior stakeholder engagement

Expertise in data governance, AI risk and cloud‑based environments

Experience supporting major transformation and crisis readiness

Desirable

$200k + Super
Role Overview

We're hiring a Senior Data Engineer with strong Snowflake and banking experience to support a major transformation program focused on migrating to Adobe Experience Platform and modernising data capabilities.

This role is critical in building high-quality, scalable data pipelines and data products that power customer engagement, analytics, and real-time decisioning.

Key Responsibilities
Design and build scalable data pipelines and data marts using Snowflake
Perform complex data transformation and data wrangling across multiple sources
Enable high-quality data for Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) and downstream use cases
Work with structured and unstructured data to support customer data platforms and marketing use cases
Collaborate with architects and Adobe specialists to ensure data is optimised for activation and personalization
Implement best practices in data engineering, data quality, and performance optimisation
Contribute to automation, orchestration, and continuous improvement of data workflows
Key Requirements
Strong hands-on experience with:
Snowflake
SQL (advanced level)
Python for data engineering
Proven experience as a Senior Data Engineer in banking or financial services
Strong understanding of:
Data modelling and data pipelines
Complex data environments (e.g. core banking, credit, mortgages, reporting)
Experience working with large-scale, enterprise data platforms
Highly Desirable
Experience with:
Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) or Adobe Analytics
Java (for orchestration / comms domain exposure)
Experience building data products for customer analytics or marketing use cases
What They're Looking For
A hands-on senior engineer who can own delivery

A candidate who can bring best practices and thought leadership
Ability to operate in a transformation environment with legacy-to-modern migration

If you feel you possess the relevant skills to the above, apply now with most updated CV!

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

What You’ll Be Doing

 

We’re looking for a curious and proactive Senior Advisor - First Line Risk to help us strengthen our enterprise-wide risk capability. You’ll sit at the heart of our Legal, Risk and Compliance team, partnering with leaders across two iconic businesses - Compare the Market and iSelect - to deliver smart, practical risk initiatives that genuinely lift how we operate.

 

With your focus squarely set on first line risk, you will: 

 

       Strengthen and maintain the Risk Management Framework
       Provide hands-on risk advisory support, turning complex inputs into clear, decision-ready outcomes
       Partner with business leaders to conduct risk self-assessments
       Help develop, evolve and maintain risk frameworks, standards and policies
       Oversee control testing and remediation to ensure risks are managed effectively
       Turn risk data and trends into clear insights and smart, targeted actions
        Lead engaging risk workshops that build understanding and support a positive risk culture

You’ll be joining a small, growing team in a developing risk environment, where there’s plenty of opportunity to shape how things are done and lots to build and improve.  This role is best suited to someone self-driven who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, is comfortable with ambiguity and thrives in an agile, evolving environment with lots of meaningful work to tackle.

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

 

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Free coffee on Mondays
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program

 

What Sets You Apart

 

You’re someone who brings energy, curiosity and a practical approach to risk - without overcomplicating things.  You’ll also have:

 

3 - 5 years’ experience in a risk-related role (regulator, fin services or similar)
Good understanding of APRA prudential standards
Comfortable working with risk frameworks and knowing how to make them work in real life
Confident building relationships with senior stakeholders, collaborating and influencing outcomes
Experience building risk policies from the ground up and supporting policy development through early stages
A continuous-improvement mindset - you like making things clearer, simpler and better
Strong communicator who can explain things clearly (verbal and written) and bring people on the journey

Interested?  Let’s chat!
