This role is available to candidates with unlimited Australian working rights only. Sponsorship is not available.  

 

Compare the Market and iSelect are committed to protecting your privacy. All applications and personal information are kept confidential and securely stored in line with Australian privacy laws, used only for recruitment purposes, and not shared with third parties outside the Auto and General Holdings Group without your consent.

Wolters Kluwer’s Tax & Accounting division in APAC delivers authoritative political, tax and legal content to professional services firms and corporations across the region. The way these firms consume, integrate, and apply that content is changing rapidly. AI-powered workflows, agent-based systems, and new integration patterns like MCP (Model Context Protocol) are reshaping how professional content gets used in practice.

We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own the development of the next iteration of our content offerings. Champion innovation, customer-centricity, and operational excellence to deliver market-leading expert solutions for legal, accounting, and political information professionals in a market where technology and competition are ever-changing. You will drive customer and business value by leading the strategy, development, and evolution of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament, ensuring alignment to wider commercial strategies to drive revenue and renewals.

Key Responsibilities
Product Innovation

Work closely with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, to understand Wolters Kluwer’s strategic and commercial objectives and the evolving needs of tax, legal and political information markets.

Define, communicate and lead a compelling and innovative product vision and strategy that aligns with those objectives and needs, and delivers on our financial goals.

Champion the integration of emerging technologies to enhance product value and competitive differentiation, driving product adoption, usage and revenue growth.

Stay current on AI delivery patterns: LLM integration, retrieval-augmented generation, MCP, agentic workflows. Use that knowledge to drive innovation that doesn’t merely keep up but leapfrogs the competition. 
 

Customer & Market Insight

Deeply understand customer segments, pain points and workflow through direct customer engagement, research and data analysis in collaboration with the Go-To-Market (GTM) team. Run client discovery to understand use cases and business scenarios to inspire innovative product development, ensuring initiatives are grounded in validated customer problems, not assumptions or technology‑led solutions.

Use qualitative and quantitative insights to validate desirability, viability, and feasibility of new product ideas and enhancements before they enter the roadmap.

Ensure all new product ideas and enhancements drive commercial objectives. Sequence and prioritize client requests: what’s achievable now with existing infrastructure, what requires new investment, what needs a phased approach.

Understand market trends and competitor insights provided by the GTM team and how they impact current and future product development. Use those insights to ideate and innovate solutions to bring to market to drive revenue goals.
 

Product Development & Delivery

In consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions and the Commercial Manager (R&L), own the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and MVP definition through to launch, ongoing optimisation, and eventual sunset, working closely with Product Owners.

Develop and maintain outcome-based product roadmaps to prioritise initiatives that drive measurable customer and commercial impact.

Ensure robust release strategies, including beta programmes, phased rollouts, and feedback loops.

Manage both an internal team of product specialists and a remote team of developers.

Develop and maintain relationships with WK’s global technology and development teams and the North American product teams to identify technology and product developments that may be adapted for the APAC market.

Collaborate cross-functionally with design, engineering, content, sales and GTM teams to deliver high-quality releases, launches and customer communications.

Conduct internal and customer-facing webinars to demonstrate product features and functionality. 
 

Commercial & Customer Success

Support the Commercial Manager (R&L) in annual, quarterly and monthly outcome-based planning, preparing business cases to secure internal capital funding for strategic initiatives (in consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions). Manage the capex and opex budget to maximize returns.

Work with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, and Commercial Manager (R&L) to set, track and report on clear success metrics (customer adoption, retention, NPS, revenue, etc) for all initiatives with accountability for closing the loop between delivery, customer outcomes, and commercial performance.

Monitor product performance, proactively identifying opportunities for improvement and intervening as needed.

Partner with Customer Success, Sales, and Marketing to ensure go‑to‑market readiness, support effective product launches, and drive sustained adoption, engagement, and customer advocacy. 

Manage regular upstream reporting requirements with the Commercial Manager (R&L) incorporating customer insight and adoption learnings alongside financial performance.
 

Team Leadership, Collaboration & Stakeholder Management

Foster a high-performing, collaborative product team culture, ensuring alignment to vision, goals, and values.

Engage with stakeholders across the business, including executive sponsors, content leaders, technology, and commercial teams to ensure product development is aligned with strategic goals.

Work collaboratively with commercial leaders in the software portfolio to identify innovation opportunities and integrate content solutions into software products. 
 

What Success Looks Like: 

Wolters Kluwer APAC is acknowledged as a market leader in delivering innovative solutions.

Customers and stakeholders clearly understand the value proposition and strategic direction of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament.

Roadmaps are executed effectively and on time, with the team focused on initiatives that drive renewal and new sales.

Product success is defined, measured, and communicated, with continuous improvement based on data and feedback. 
 

About You:

Proven experience in product strategy and management, ideally in B2B SaaS or a professional services technology space. Tax, accounting, or legal domain experience is a plus but not required if you bring strong professional services or fintech context.

Experience with AI-driven product development and AI implementation. You can talk to a client’s technology leadership about LLMs, APIs, retrieval-augmented generation, and agent architectures without reading from a slide. Familiarity with MCP or a track record of picking up new technical integration patterns quickly.

You’ve operated in a global, matrixed organization and know how to get things done when your success depends on teams in other time zones and other reporting lines. Demonstrated ability to inspire and lead a team when delivering complex projects.

You’re comfortable when the playbook doesn’t exist yet - clients are asking for things they can’t fully describe. You help them figure out what they actually need and then find a path to deliver it.

You have a passion for innovation which is tempered by strong strategic thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills, and a commercial approach. You are flexible in your approach and take initiative rather than wait to be asked. 

Demonstrated ability to define and articulate a clear strategic vision in ambiguous environments, and to translate that vision into commercially viable, revenue‑generating deliverables that drive sustainable business growth.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to engage with customers and multiple stakeholders in a complex business. 

Our Values

Wolters Kluwer continuously strives for an inclusive company culture in which we attract, develop, and retain high-performing, productive, engaged, and diverse talent to deliver on our strategy. As a global company, having a diverse workforce from different backgrounds, nationalities, races, genders, gender identities, ages, sexual orientations, physical disabilities, religions, expertise, and talents is of the utmost importance.

We pride ourselves on our culture, which promotes inclusion, accessibility and flexible working arrangements.

Culture and Benefits

We care for our people and a part of that we offer:

Flexible Working Arrangement – Work from Anywhere and Hybrid Working (promoting work life balance)

Learning and Development opportunities

Access to health and wellness programs

Parental leave benefits that exceed legislative requirements

The opportunity to work within a global organization with experienced leaders

You can learn more about what we do by visiting our:
 

Website : Wolters Kluwer Australia | Wolters Kluwer

Meet the People: How the People of Wolters Kluwer Strive to Be The Difference | Wolters Kluwer

Our Interview Practices


To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Job Title: Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the Company 

SA Water is a successful, modern water utility wholly owned by the Government of South Australia. We employ more than 1,700 people across a broad range of disciplines who operate more than $14 billion worth of infrastructure to deliver essential water and sewerage services to more than 1.8 million South Australians.

We operate in a geographically and climatically diverse service area, with sites and locations from Ceduna through to Port Augusta, the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands, across to the Riverland, down to Mount Gambier, and most places in between.

Our strength and expertise have been shaped by our history. For more than 165 years, South Australians have depended on us to innovate and deliver efficient and sustainable water services. With nationally and internationally recognised industry expertise, we’re focused on understanding and meeting our customers’ expectations.

As well as safely delivering smart and reliable services for our customers of today, we innovate to shape the water solutions of the future that will support thriving economies and healthy communities.

We are driven by our values; we value our people, we do work that matters, we keep it real, we know that our work has impact and we are future focussed. Staying true to our values ensures that we consistently deliver positive outcomes for all South Australians.

About the job  

The Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead plays a pivotal role in shaping how data insights and AI are used to drive smarter decision-making across Growth. Leading a small team of data experts and contractors, this role will work as a Data Product Owner and is responsible for delivering high-quality analytics, building strong data foundations and enabling scalable, AI driven solutions.  

Acting as the key interface between our transformation partner and internal teams, you’ll ensure initiatives are grounded in our data environment and aligned to business priorities.  

This is a hands-on leadership role focused on simplifying complexity, automating processes, accelerating data product delivery and embedding efficient repeatable ways of working that create real impact.  

This is a max-term opportunity until June 2027. 

About the Person

You’re a pragmatic and curious leader who understands how data and analytics come together in practice. With a strong background in analytics, reporting, data engineering, you’re comfortable navigating complex environments and translating between technical teams and business stakeholders.  

You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you have genuine interest in its potential and how it can be applied to solve real problems. You led from the front, bringing others with you, and are driven by improving how things work, whether that through automation, improve processes or deliver continuous value through smarter use of insights.  

You’re equally comfortable setting direction and rolling up your sleeves to make it happen.  

About Growth

The Growth group is at the heart of shaping South Australia’s future. This team plays a key role in supporting the state’s housing and infrastructure growth by delivering trusted water services that unlock land for development, create thriving new communities, and sustain existing regions. With a strong focus on single-point accountability, they ensure efficiency, consistent decision-making, and seamless connections for our customers. By bringing expertise and innovation together, they help drive smarter solutions and stronger partnerships that support South Australia’s growth for generations to come.

In addition, your responsibilities will include: 

Fostering trust with our people, customers and community by providing expert advice and service that ensure we effectively communicate and engage audiences with our business strategy and its delivery across South Australia, every day. 

You will: 

Lead, mentor, coach, guide the team to deliver data driven insights faster. 
Ensure the intelligence analysts, contractors and other personnel within the team achieve business objectives and goals within agreed timeframes. 
Provide timely guidance, feedback, coaching and training to help team members strengthen specific knowledge/skill areas needed to accomplish a task or solve a problem.
Lead initiatives to improve the maturity of domain data modelling, Growth Intelligence and Transformation.  
Review and determine suitability of new, and emerging AI technology, solutions/algorithms/models and recommend to be adopted by the Growth Group. 
Collaborate with teams across Growth, Planning, Systems, Customer and Technology to support integrated planning and intelligence data product delivery. 

Your success in this role will come from: 

Tertiary qualification in Data, AI, Information Technology, Business, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in supporting the implementation of data solutions that align with business objectives. 
Effective communication and people skills including an ability to collaborate to deliver improved business outcomes.
Intermediate skill level in Microsoft Azure, Collibra, Microsoft SQL, data integration, data modelling, and data architecture.  
Ability to lead the team manage multiple tasks and deadlines within a continuously improving organization. 

To apply, click 'Apply'. Please submit and complete the application form, upload a cover letter and current resume. For enquiries please contact Steph Haines from the Talent Acquisition team steph.haines@sawater.com.au

Further information can be found in the attached PD - Growth Intelligence - Analytics & AI Lead

Business Analyst - Adelaide - M365 uplift

Location: Adelaide
Contract length: 12 months (definite extensions)
Daily rate: $750-900 per day including super

About

This organisation is undertaking a major uplift of its Microsoft 365 environment, focused on strengthening governance, improving content management, and enabling secure, scalable collaboration.

The current landscape is fragmented, with inconsistent processes and legacy platforms limiting efficiency, compliance, and the ability to adopt emerging capabilities such as GenAI. This program is designed to modernise the environment through clear governance frameworks, streamlined content management practices, and enhanced information security controls.

The role will play a key part in shaping how information is managed, accessed, and protected across the business, supporting both immediate operational improvements and long-term scalability.

Responsibilities

Lead business analysis activities across a Microsoft 365 governance and content management uplift program
Facilitate workshops with business and technical stakeholders to gather, challenge, and refine requirements
Capture current ("as is") processes and design future ("to be") states aligned to best practice
Translate business needs into clear, structured requirements and supporting artefacts
Identify opportunities for process improvement, standardisation, and automation
Support the development of governance frameworks, information architecture, and content management models
Contribute to business case development, benefits identification, and value realisation tracking
Work closely with delivery teams to ensure requirements are understood and implemented effectively
Support project lifecycle activities including planning, delivery, and operational handover

Criteria

Strong core Business Analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process mapping, and stakeholder engagement
Experience working within complex IT environments, ideally on enterprise-wide transformation initiatives
Proven ability to facilitate workshops and communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Experience with process improvement methodologies and identifying inefficiencies
Strong understanding of Microsoft 365 ecosystem (e.g. SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive, Power Platform)
Knowledge of information management practices, including governance, data classification, and retention
Experience working in Agile or hybrid delivery environments
Familiarity with regulatory and compliance considerations relating to data and information management
Ability to operate across both strategic and hands-on delivery activities

Why Apply

Opportunity to contribute to a high-impact, enterprise-wide transformation program
Work on modernising collaboration and information management practices at scale
Exposure to governance, security, and emerging technologies including GenAI readiness
Engage with a broad range of stakeholders across business and technology
Join a collaborative environment that values initiative, accountability, and continuous improvement


If the above seems like a good fit, please apply or email karim.romdhane@precisionsourcing.com.au and we will be in touch if your profile is suitable!

A lot of data science roles talk about impact.
This one measures it.

You’ll work on AI and GenAI features that sit directly in front of customers. Not internal dashboards. Not experiments that never ship. Real product functionality, where success is defined by whether it improves the experience and holds up in the real world.

This team is embedding ML and LLM capabilities into products used by thousands of customers. That means real constraints, real trade offs, and real accountability. You’ll operate in domains where correctness, trust, and compliance matter just as much as innovation.

They’ve moved past asking “can we build this?”

WorkCover Queensland is entering an exciting new chapter, with a renewed strategic focus and strong executive leadership driving our 2030 Strategy. As we deliver an enterprise-wide transformation, strong data management and governance will underpin our ability to make a lasting difference for Queenslander employers and employees.

Building WorkCover’s Data Future

As part of this transformation, WorkCover is establishing a new Data Office to uplift data maturity across the organisation. This collaborative, customer centric and outcomes focused function brings together specialist leads across governance and quality, working as one integrated team.

Moving away from a traditional centralised model, the Data Office operates through partnership and collaboration, embedding with business and delivery teams to enable trusted, high quality data that supports both business as usual excellence and future capability.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Manager, Data Office, the Lead, Data Office Quality is accountable for ensuring WorkCover’s data is trusted, fit for purpose and aligned to organisational goals.

As the organisation’s data quality authority, you set direction, provide expert guidance and influence how quality is embedded throughout the data lifecycle. You work across business units and transformation teams to uplift data maturity, enable confident decision making and support WorkCover’s move towards a more reliable, future ready data ecosystem.

This role exists to build confidence in data, reduce risk and ensure quality is not an afterthought, but an embedded capability across the organisation.

In this role you will:

Lead day to day data quality operations, ensuring reliable and trusted data across transformation initiatives.
Define, embed and maintain data quality standards, frameworks and controls.
Act as the central escalation point for data quality issues, risks and exceptions.
Monitor, profile and report on data quality, identifying trends, risks and systemic issues.
Lead root cause analysis and guide teams to implement sustainable remediation.
Partner with delivery teams to ensure data products meet quality standards prior to release.
Collaborate with business, analytics and transformation teams to drive consistent quality outcomes.
Drive continuous improvement and uplift data quality maturity across the organisation.

About You

You are an experienced data quality leader with a strong transformation mindset and a passion for building confidence in data. You thrive on collaboration, bring structure to complexity and influence outcomes through expertise and engagement. You bring:

Extensive experience leading data quality in complex or regulated environments.
Strong knowledge of data quality frameworks, governance, privacy and regulatory controls.
Proven ability to diagnose issues and deliver sustainable remediation.
Confidence embedding quality practices into BAU operations and transformation initiatives.
Excellent communication skills across technical and non technical audiences.
A collaborative, coaching leadership style with a continuous improvement mindset.

Why Join Us

Our vision is to be the best workers’ compensation insurer, making a positive difference to people’s lives and keeping Queenslanders working. At WorkCover, we deliver with heart, own our outcomes, work best together and stay open to what’s possible - and we’re looking for people who share these values

When you join us, you’ll benefit from:

A purpose driven organisation where your work genuinely makes a positive impact on Queenslanders’ lives
A collaborative and supportive team environment that values empathy, trust and working together
Hybrid work arrangements that support flexibility and work–life balance
Personalised learning and development opportunities to support your growth and career progression
A holistic health and wellbeing program designed to support you both professionally and personally
Industry competitive salaries
A generous superannuation scheme, including co contribution
Study assistance to support ongoing education and professional development

Job Title: Solutions Engineer / Pre Sales Consultant (Risk & Audit)New
Ad Text:
Position Overview:

This role is ideal for someone from a Risk, Audit, or Compliance background who enjoys working closely with customers, solving complex problems, and presenting thoughtful, high-impact solutions in a commercial environment.

As a Solutions Engineer at Diligent, you will partner closely with Sales to help organisations understand how our platform can transform the way they manage risk, audit, and compliance. You’ll act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, translating risk and audit challenges into clear, compelling solutions that drive real business value.

If you enjoy combining domain expertise, storytelling, and customer interaction and want to move beyond traditional internal risk or audit roles, this is a strong next step.

Key Responsibilities:

Customer & Commercial Partnership


Partner closely with Sales to support the full pre-sales lifecycle, helping customers understand how Diligent solves real-world risk and audit challenges.

Deliver tailored, high-impact software demonstrations that clearly articulate business value not just product features.

Engage confidently with senior leaders across Audit, Risk, Compliance, IT, and Governance functions.

Translate customer pain points into clear solution narratives that support deal progression and closure.

Solution Design & Thought Leadership


Design and present solution architectures that align with customer objectives and regulatory environments.

Articulate the cross-platform value of the Diligent Platform and how it supports integrated GRC programs.

Collaborate with Product teams to provide customer and domain feedback on product enhancements.

Support marketing initiatives such as webinars, industry events, and thought-leadership content.

Domain Expertise


Apply practical knowledge of Audit, Risk Management, and IT Compliance in customer conversations.

Leverage familiarity with industry frameworks such as COSO ERM, ISO 31000, NIST RMF, and related standards.

Demonstrate how modern GRC platforms enable risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and mitigation at scale.

Required Skills/Experience

Experience in Risk, Audit, Compliance, or GRC, either in a customer-facing role or within industry.

Experience working with SaaS solutions or strong interest in moving into a commercial, customer-facing environment.

Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics.

Comfortable partnering with Sales and contributing to commercial outcomes.

Curious, self-driven, and motivated to continuously deepen both technical and domain knowledge.

#LI-SM1

#LI-Hybrid

About Us

Diligent is the AI leader in governance, risk and compliance (GRC) SaaS solutions, helping more than 1 million users and 700,000 board members to clarify risk and elevate governance. The Diligent One Platform gives practitioners, the C-Suite and the board a consolidated view of their entire GRC practice so they can more effectively manage risk, build greater resilience and make better decisions, faster. 

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast.  Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking.  Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity.  The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together.  At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Learn more at diligent.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook

What Diligent Offers You 

Creativity is ingrained in our culture. We are innovative collaborators by nature. We thrive in exploring how things can be differently both in our internal processes and to help our clients
We care about our people. Diligent offers a flexible work environment, global days of service, comprehensive health benefits, meeting free days, generous time off policy and wellness programs to name a few
We have teams all over the world. We may be headquartered in New York City, but we have office hubs in Washington D.C., Vancouver, London, Galway, Budapest, Munich, Bengaluru, Singapore, and Sydney.
Diversity is important to us. Growing, maintaining and promoting a diverse team is a top priority for us. We foster and encourage diversity through our Employee Resource Groups and provide access to resources and education to support the education of our team, facilitate dialogue, and foster understanding.

Diligent created the modern governance movement. Our world-changing idea is to empower leaders with the technology, insights and connections they need to drive greater impact and accountability – to lead with purpose. Our employees are passionate, smart, and creative people who not only want to help build the software company of the future, but who want to make the world a more sustainable, equitable and better place. 

Headquartered in New York, Diligent has offices in Washington D.C.,  London, Galway, Budapest, Vancouver, Bengaluru, Munich, Singapore and Sydney.   To foster strong collaboration and connection, this role will follow a hybrid work model. If you are within a commuting distance to one of our Diligent office locations, you will be expected to work onsite at least 50% of the time. We believe that in-person engagement helps drive innovation, teamwork, and a strong sense of community.

We are a drug free workplace. Diligent is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, pregnancy, childbirth, physical disability, mental disability, age, military status, protected veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner or civil union status, gender (including sex stereotyping and gender identity or expression), medical condition (including, but not limited to, cancer related or HIV/AIDS related), genetic information, or sexual orientation in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Job Description


Sydney or Melbourne 
2nd LOD

Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Head of Information & Transformation Risk works with the General Manager, Operational Risk & Resilience to design, develop, implement, maintain and monitor Rest’s Enterprise Risk Management Framework (including assurance activities) as it relates to Information Technology, Information Security and Project Delivery risks, demonstrating and embodying a sound risk culture, and meeting Rest’s legislative and regulatory obligations.

Lead and manage the Information & Transformation Risk Team to develop capability, build a high performing team, foster collaboration, and increase engagement.
Provide independent and effective challenge to Data, Technology & Delivery (DTD) teams and broader organisation over Rest activities and decisions that may affect Rest’s information & transformation risks, this includes risks associated with information technology, cyber security, project delivery and data.
Lead the design, implementation and maintenance of appropriate frameworks, processes and capability supporting the management and assurance over information & transformation risks at Rest, aligning to regulatory and legislative obligations, and Rest’s strategic objectives.
Lead Second Line controls testing, assurance and risk reviews related to DTD teams, Rest’s project portfolio and other broader enterprise business activities as required, to ensure adequate controls are implemented commensurate with risks, vulnerabilities, and threats.
Prepare and present regular risk reporting to Executive forums and Board/Committees to support delivery of business outcomes.

Qualifications


Current certifications relevant to Information Technology or Cyber Security, such as CISSP, CISA or others as relevant (highly desired)  
Experience in senior risk, compliance or audit roles (primarily focused on Information Technology, Cyber Security and/or Project Delivery)
Experience leading risk advisory and assurance over large projects and transformation programs
Technical knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in risk management, technology, cyber security, project delivery, and artificial intelligence
Knowledge of frameworks and standards relevant to risk management, technology, cyber security, project delivery, and artificial intelligence (e.g. COBIT, NIST, ISO 27001, Agile, Voluntary AI Safety Standard)
Knowledge of the superannuation industry and legislative / regulatory obligations relevant to risk management, technology, and cyber security, including SPS 220, CPS 230, CPS 234, SOCI
Experience drafting high quality papers for Senior Leaders, Executives, Boards and Committees

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave 
Linkedin Learning 
Income Protection Insurance 
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours) 
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks 

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.

Rest is committed to creating a flexible work environment and culture that embraces diversity, equity, and inclusion - where people feel welcome, safe to be themselves and inspired to do their best.

We value the different backgrounds, lived experiences and abilities our diverse team brings. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all ages, cultural backgrounds, faiths, gender identities, sexual orientations and thinking styles. This includes people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander peoples and those with disrupted work history due to career or other breaks.

Please note only people with the right to work in Australia will be considered.

Job Title: Head of Internal Communications and Employee Experience
Ad Text:
Pivotal role in delivering Insignia Financials’ Vision 2030
Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane Location
Reports directly to the Chief Customer Officer, Advice & Customer

The opportunity to join our team

Excellent opportunity to join our Advice and Customer Division as Head of Internal Communications & Employee Experience. This is a critical role in delivering Insignia Financial’s Vision 2030 — to become Australia’s leading and most efficient wealth management company. 

This role shapes how our people understand, experience and contribute to Insignia’s strategy.

It is accountable for building a clear, trusted internal narrative that connects employees to our customer obsession, simplification agenda, technology transformation and growth ambitions across superannuation, advice, platforms and asset management. 

Key Responsibilities:

Lead and develop a high‑performing internal communications team to design and deliver IFL’s enterprise internal communications strategy
Act as a trusted adviser to the CEO and Executive Team on internal narrative, tone and messaging priorities
Lead CEO communications, including town halls, enterprise updates and key moments of engagement 
Accountable for divisional communications strategies and rhythms 
Translate enterprise‑wide strategy and change initiatives into clear, compelling internal messages (e.g. rollout and embedment of AI tools)
Establish and maintain enterprise‑wide communication standards, governance and communication rhythms 
Build concise leader communication toolkits that enable clear, engaging and consistent leader communication
Use compelling storytelling to translate strategy into human, engaging messages that bring IFL’s purpose to life
Build leader capability in storytelling, equipping leaders to communicate with authenticity, clarity and impact to drive alignment and action
Facilitate IFL’s communications community of practice  
In partnership with Organisational Development, communicate the end‑to‑end employee experience ensuring alignment to IFL’s culture, values and strategic priorities
Lead and deliver targeted initiatives that improve priority aspects of the employee experience, using engagement data and insights to measure impact and guide continuous improvement
Drive the creation of positive, locally relevant employee experiences across all IFL locations through the development and execution of office engagement plans
Plan and oversee engaging employee events and experiences that strengthen connection, inclusion and a sense of belonging across the organisation

What you will bring:

10+ years’ experience in senior internal communications and/or employee experience leadership roles within complex organisations
Experience within regulated financial services, with an understanding of risk, governance and stakeholder complexity
Demonstrated ability to design, deliver and embed enterprise strategies that drive measurable employee and business outcomes
Proven expertise in transformation communications, including leading narrative, engagement and alignment through significant organisational change 
Highly effective executive communicator and influencer, with the ability to advise, challenge and support senior leaders with a calm and credible presence
Thrives in fast‑paced, evolving environments, bringing clarity and stability through periods of ambiguity and transformation
Strong people leader with a track record of building, leading and developing high‑performing, engaged teams
Highly collaborative operator, able to partner effectively across functions and influence without authority to deliver enterprise outcomes

Make your move. Make your mark.

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people do more with their money. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we’ll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. Here, you’ll be making a positive impact in people’s lives straightaway – and every day. The first point of contact for our clients whenever they need support.

Along the way, you’ll be free to learn new skills, grow, and get great things done. Around here, we like to call it “putting our fingerprints on the future”.

Showing up for every Australian

We’re one of Australia’s leading financial wellbeing organisations, helping people feel good about their money. This starts by helping our people feel good about working here. That means flexibility. Plenty of opportunity. And celebrating all the unique things that make you, you – what matters to you, matters to us.

To Apply

Read more about why you should join our team https://www.insigniafinancial.com.au/careers/working-with-us/why-join-our-team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Technical Writer - Defence (Baseline Cleared)

Immediate Start | Pathway into Procurement Analyst

Our client is a high-performing boutique consulting firm supporting major programs within the Australian defence industry. They are seeking an experienced Technical Writer to join a project team delivering SOPs, training content, guidance material and process documentation in a complex Federal Government / Defence environment.

This is a hands-on role for someone who can work with subject matter experts, analyse business processes and system outputs, and turn complex information into clear, practical documentation that supports new ways of working.

For the right person, this role offers a genuine pathway into a Procurement Analyst position following the initial engagement period.

About the role

You will work closely with SMEs and project stakeholders to develop high-quality documentation, training material and supporting project artefacts within a complex enterprise environment.

This role will suit someone who can work autonomously, communicate well with a broad stakeholder group, and produce polished, structured outputs in a fast-paced project setting.

What you’ll be doing

Develop and finalise standard operating procedures, guidance documents, user guides and process documentation

Create training materials, eLearning content, presentations and workshop packs for a range of user groups

Liaise with SMEs to gather, validate and structure information for review, approval and publishing

Review business processes, workflows and system outputs to identify documentation gaps, inefficiencies and training needs

Support project delivery through governance, change and stakeholder reporting artefacts

Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing project team

What we’re looking for
Essential

Current AGSVA Baseline Security Clearance

Ability to start immediately

Minimum 5 years’ experience in Technical Writing, business/process analysis, or closely related documentation-focused roles

Strong ability to translate complex information into clear, accurate content for both technical and non-technical audiences

Proven ability to work independently, manage competing priorities and engage effectively with stakeholders

Strong written communication skills, attention to detail and document quality control capability

Exposure to procurement functions, processes, or support activities within the defence sector.

Highly regarded

Previous experience in a defence.

Exposure to ERP, business process documentation, SOP development or systems-based project work.

Background in procurement, commercial analysis, supply chain, logistics or analyst-style roles.

Interest in transitioning into a Procurement Analyst pathway over time.

Why apply

Immediate-start opportunity in a Defence environment

Meaningful work across documentation, training, stakeholder engagement and process analysis

A genuine opportunity to transition into a Procurement Analyst role for a strong performer

A role suited to someone who wants more than pure document writing and is looking to broaden their career over time

Apply now

If you hold a current Baseline clearance, are available to start immediately, and are looking for a Technical Writer role with genuine progression into analysis and procurement, we’d like to hear from you.
